---
title: "Raymond Parcon"
about:
  template: broadside
  links:
    - text: LinkedIn
      url: "https://www.linkedin.com/in/rayparcon"
    - text: Instagram
      icon: instagram
      url: "https://www.instagram.com/ry.parcs/"
    - text: GitHub
      icon: github
      url: "https://github.com/rayparcon"
    - text: Email me
      url: "mailto:rhparcon@uga.edu"
    - text: "ORCID"
      icon: orcid
      url: "https://orcid.org/0000-0002-3379-3698" 
    - text: "Google Scholar"
      icon: google-scholar
      url: "https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=zDKlF7kAAAAJ&hl=en"
    - text: Resume
      url: "resume.pdf"

---

## Hi, I'm Ray 👋🏽

I'm a Ph.D. candidate in the Department of Plant Pathology at the University of Georgia. My interest in host-pathogen interactions led me to work as a graduate research assistant in the Smith Lab. Together with my advisors, [Dr. Shavannor Smith](https://plantpath.caes.uga.edu/people/faculty/shavannor-smith.html) and [Dr. James Buck](https://plantpath.caes.uga.edu/people/faculty/james-buck.html), we study the genetic diversity of <em>Cercospora sojina</em>, the causal fungus of frogeye leaf spot disease in soybeans, and its implications for host resistance.

## This site is currently under construction, but you can find my research papers, presentations, and blog posts here:[rayparcon.github.io/ray-bio](https://rayparcon.github.io/ray-bio)
